mission.title: alertmanager: Telegram can't parse entities after message truncation with parse_mode="HTML"
mission.description: Telegram can't parse entities after message truncation with parse_mode="HTML". This issue affects 23+ users.

steps.title: Identify alertmanager troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your alertmanager deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager -n alertmanager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review alertmanager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant alertmanager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl get configmap -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=alertmanager
```
**What did you do?**
Used Telegram integration with parse_mode="HTML"

**What did you expect to see?**
Alert notification in Telegram chat

**What did you see instead? Under which circumstances?**
Alert notification wasn't sent to chat

* System

steps.title: Apply the fix for Telegram can't parse entities after message truncation with…
steps.description:
I used [this workaround](https://github.com/pando85/homelab/blob/a8fd225df671dffa9b1b619d495ff249afecc99c/system/monitoring/resources/alertmanager-telegram-template.yaml#L30-L43) until we have a better solution:

```gotemplate
        {{- $alertsLen := (len .Alerts) -}}
        {{ range .Alerts
```yaml
global:
  resolve_timeout: 3m
templates:
- '/etc/alertmanager/templates/*.tmpl'
receivers:
- name: alerts-test1
  telegram_configs:
  - api_url: https://api.telegram.org
    bot_token: 1657974151:BBBO9Iggg_Tif04Tg9asdfSuR83niyNOSHE  # has been changed for security reasons
    chat_id: -10075044612354    # has been changed for security reasons
    disable_notifications: false
    message: '{{ template "telegram.html.message" . }}'
    parse_mode: HTML
    send_resolved: true

route:
  group_by:
  - alertname
  group_interval: 30s
  group_wait: 15s
  receiver: alerts-test1
  repeat_interval: 1d
```

steps.title: Confirm Telegram can't parse entities after message… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager -n alertmanager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n alertmanager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

codeSnippets:
global:

templates:

receivers:

  telegram_configs:

    chat_id: -10075044612354    # has been changed for security reasons
    disable_notifications: false
    message: '{{ template "telegram.html.message" . }}'
    parse_mode: HTML
    send_resolved: true

route:
